title: ilex tolucana var. genuina
intro: Ilex tolucana var. genuina, also known as Mexican Holly or Guayacanillo, is a small evergreen tree native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Aquifoliaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Ilex tolucana var. genuina include its ability to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
